title: Lightly Citrusy Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A dense and flavorful Tunisian dish featuring lamb, citrus, and pink food coloring, perfect for a post-workout meal. This halal-friendly recipe is made with brine-dried ingredients and is sure to satisfy your hunger.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the dried lemon peel, pink food coloring, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the chopped brine-dried apricots and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
